design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate platform stages, collect gems to progress, avoid hazards and enemies
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Platforming precision under time pressure with limited energy
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Experience the Jungle Book story as Mowgli navigating the jungle environment
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Port-based design that mirrors Genesis version rather than establishing unique NES identity; requires gem collection gating for progression

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple lives system present; infinite lives available via debug menu
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpointing; must complete full level to progress
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency via extensive debug menu: infinite energy, infinite lives, infinite time, single gem mode, and solid bottom mode all accessible through sound test sequence

progression.unlock_mechanism: Gem collection required to complete stages; shovel item required to access bonus levels (can be bypassed via debug menu)
replayability_hooks: Hidden level select accessible via debug menu
Bonus levels gated behind shovel collection
Multiple difficulty modifiers available for custom playthroughs

world_building_techniques: Disney film IP adaptation provides context

danger_signals: Game became notorious as basis for pirated Lion King hack with inappropriate content modifications
environmental_storytelling: Jungle environment reflects source material setting

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (Jungle Book NES page)
